I II III IV Jfi:I T. <rqr ff qrrr crfufrtfr{?cqF6-drfts (crcftc{ctt 1 3,00,000 EctE 10,00,000 Fci s 15,000/- Ec+ 2 1o,0o,o0o -qr + 50,00,000 F,ct rd 30,000/- -rrq 3. 50,00,000 {ct + 1,00,00,000 Ec} fi 40,000/- 6.m -- 7 of 15 -- 8 THE CAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY IPARr II-SEC. 3(i)] 4 100,00,000 ncq+ 3,oo,0o,ooo nci s 50,000/- ntrq 5. 3,00,00,000 nct + srFi6 75,000/- nct F. 9-6001 1 (06)/201201 6-g<ft-a-1 ; ;1q-a1r11 sr. ?Tfi-q qFr, eg< aE-a atr F?rrff e<rf+rr MINISTRY Otr LAW AND JUSTICE (DepartmeDt of Legal Affairs) NOTINCATION Ncw Delhi, the 3rd July, 2018 G.S.R.606(E).-ln exercise of the powers confened by sub-section (2) of section 2lA read with sub-section ( | ) of section l2A of the Commercial Cou(s Act, 2015 (4 of 2016). the Cenral Government hereby makes the fbllowing rules. namely:- l Short aide and commencement. - ( I ) These rules may be called the Commercial Courts (Prejnstitution Mediation and Settlement) Rules, 2018. (2) They shall come into force on the date of thcir publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Delinitions.- (I) In these rules unless the conlcxt othc ise requircs,- (a) "Act" means thc Commercial Courts Act,2015 (4 of 20t6); (b) "applicant" means a p€rson who approachcs the Authority under rulc 3 for thc initiation of mediation Process: (c) "Authority" means the Authority notified by the Central Governmcnt under sut!.section (2) of scction I 2A of the Act; (d) "commercial dispute" means the commercial dispute as defined in clause (c) of sub-section (l) of secrion 2 of the Act; (d) "Form" meaos the Form sp€cified in the Schedule to these rules: (e) "mediatioD" means a process undertaken by a Mediator to resolve, reconcile aod settle a commelcial dispute between the pafiies thercto, (0 "Mediator" means a person empanelled by the Authority fbr conducting the mediationi G) "oppositc part/' means a pany against whom relief is sought in a commcrcial dispute; (h) "Schedule" means the Schedule appended to these rules: and (i) "settlemcnt" mcans thc settlcment of commercial dispute arrived at by the partics to thc modiation; (2) The words and expressions used and not defined in these rules, shall have the same meanings rcspectively as assigned to them in the Act or the lJgal Services Authorities Act, 1987 (39 of 1987) or in any other law for the time being in force, 3. Initiation of mediation process,- ( I ) A party to a commercial dispulc may makc an applicalion to lhc Authorily as pcr lbrm- I spccificd in Schcdulc-I, cirhcr oolinc or by Post or by hand. for ioitiation of mcdiation process under the Act along wilh ! fee ol-one thousand rupees payable to the Authority either by way of demand &aft or through online; -- 8 of 15 -- I qrq rr-qrs 3( D] qrId 6l {nTqi : 3{glqRql (2) The Authority shall. having regard to the territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction and thc nalure of conmercial dispute, issue a notice, as per Form-2 specitied in Schedule-l through a registered or speed posr and elecrronic mcans including e-mail and the likc to the opposite parly to appear and give consenl to participalc in the mcdiation process on such date not beyond a period of tcn days from thc datc of issuc ofthc said noticc. (3) Whcrc no responsc is rcceivcd from lhe oppositc party cilher by posl or b!, e-nrail, lhc Authority shall issuc a final notice to it in thc manner as specified iri sub-rule (2). (4) Wlcre the notice issued under sub-rule (3) remains unacknowledgcd or rvhcre the opposite pafly reluses to palticipate in the mediation prccess, the Authority shall treat the mediation preess to be a lon-staner and orake a rcporl as pcr Form 3 spccilicd in the Schedule-I and cndorse thc same to lhc applicant and thc oppositc pruly. (5) where the opposite party. after recciving the notice under suh-rule (2) or (3) seeks funher time for his appeannce. the Authority may. if it thinks fit, lix an olternate d e not later than teD davs lrom the dnte of receipt of such rcquest from thc opposite party. (6) Wherc the opposite party fails to appear on the date fixed under sub-rulc (5). the Aurhoriry shall treat the flediation process to be a non-starter and make a report tr this b€half os per Form 3 specilied in Schedulel irnd endorsc thc same to the applicant and the opposite pany. (7) Where both the parties to the commercial dispute appear belbre the Authority and givc consent to purticipute in lhe mediation process. the Authority shall assiSn the connnercial clisputc to a Mediator and lix a date lbr their appearance before the said Mediator. (E) Thc Anthority shall ensure that the mediation process is completed within a period of threc months tionr thc date of reccipt of application tbr prc-institution mcdiation unles$ thc period is extended for further two months with thc consent ol thc apltlrcant and thc uppo(itc pany. 4. Venue for conducting mediation.- The venue for conducting of the mediation shall be the premises of the Authority. 5. Role of Mediator.- The Mediator shall, on receipt of the assignment under sub-rule (7) ot rule 3. Iacilitate the voluntary resolution of the commercial dispute between the parties and assist them in caching a setllemeol. 6. Repr€s€ntation of paities.- A party lo a commercial dispute shall appear before the Authority or Mediator, as the cas€ may be, either personally or through his duly authorised representative or Counsel. 7.
